mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in over 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above 0.4pp, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of observed cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2% annualized during periods of rising inflation signals supply-side overheating that forces monetary tightening and depresses equity valuations.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO exceeds 2% during inflationary periods, or the negative correlation coefficient drops below -0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and corporate profit margins, creating a persistent upward bias in S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll growth over any 24-month rolling window, or mean return difference is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation breaching the 2.5% annual threshold triggers expectations of aggressive central bank rate hikes that compress equity multiples and produce negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Markets price in inflation sustainably without drawdowns, resulting in positive 20-day returns in over 65% of threshold breaches.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong GDP growth and weak retail sales indicates income concentration outside consumer channels, reducing broad market earnings visibility and generating negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The GDP-retail sales divergence fails to predict negative 60-day returns, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contraction during periods of falling unemployment signals a supply shock that disrupts production chains and forces negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally or remain flat during industrial production contractions alongside stable unemployment, invalidating the supply shock transmission channel.
